purpose: Optional bootstrap module for NVIDIA GPU support on Debian/Ubuntu/WSL2; detects GPU presence, installs nvtop, nvidia-container-toolkit, and iopaint (AI inpainting), prints platform-appropriate setup guidance, and registers manifest entries including an SDXL inpainting checkpoint shim if present.
invariants: This module is opt-in only — never in DEFAULT_GROUPS; must be activated with --with optional-gpu
Does not auto-install the CUDA toolkit or any GPU ML frameworks — those are project/container scoped
nvidia-smi detection gates GPU-specific installs (nvtop, container toolkit, guidance); iopaint installs unconditionally (works on CPU, faster on GPU)
The SDXL checkpoint shim is only written if the HuggingFace cache directory already exists
gotchas: On WSL2, nvidia-smi absence message instructs using the Windows host driver; on native Linux it points to the distro's NVIDIA package — branch determined by is_wsl()
manifest_add for nvidia-smi uses install_method 'windows-host-driver' on WSL and 'apt' on native Linux
nvidia-container-toolkit requires resetting _APT_UPDATED=0 before calling apt_install to force a fresh apt-get update after adding the NVIDIA repo
docker runtime configuration (nvidia-ctk runtime configure) is skipped if docker daemon is not running at install time
iopaint pins Pillow==9.5.0 which fails to build on Python 3.13; workaround uses 'uv tool install iopaint --override <tempfile>' where tempfile contains 'Pillow>=11.0.0' — pipx cannot be used here
iopaint does not support --version; the manifest detect command uses 'command -v iopaint' instead
uv must be installed (python group) before optional-gpu group; optional_gpu_iopaint checks 'has uv' and errors if absent
The sdxl-inpaint shim is a 'test -d' script, not a real tool — exists only so devtools check and smoke-test can verify checkpoint presence via command -v

key_functions.name: optional_gpu_install
key_functions.summary: Detects GPU via nvidia-smi, branches on is_wsl() for absence messaging, installs nvtop and container toolkit (if GPU present), installs iopaint unconditionally, prints platform-appropriate setup guide, and records manifest entries

key_functions.name: optional_gpu_iopaint
key_functions.summary: Installs iopaint via 'uv tool install --override' to work around the Pillow==9.5.0/Python 3.13 incompatibility; iopaint provides AI inpainting for object/person/background removal

key_functions.name: optional_gpu_container_toolkit
key_functions.summary: Adds the NVIDIA apt repo, installs nvidia-container-toolkit, and configures the docker runtime if the daemon is running

key_functions.name: _optional_gpu_record_sdxl_inpaint
key_functions.summary: Writes a presence-check shim for the SDXL inpainting checkpoint and registers it in the manifest if the HuggingFace cache dir exists
